task_description:
You are provided with the following network description and its current state:
Network Description: A small RIP-based mini Internet with internal routers, external zones, and a VPN overlay. Internal FRR routers form a full mesh and connect to a gateway router, with internal hosts on 10.0.X.0/24 LANs (router as default gateway). The gateway connects to several external FRR routers, each fronting a bridged server LAN in 20.0.X.0/24 that hosts Apache web servers. One of them serves a WireGuard VPN server and the web servers (1_1 and 1_2) are accessible only via the VPN. All inter-router links use /31s from 192.168.0.0/16, and RIP runs on every router for both infrastructure and LAN prefixes. One internal host (host_1) is preconfigured as a WireGuard VPN client, establishing an encrypted tunnel to the external VPN server and allowing secure access to the external web services.
Hosts: host_1, host_2
Web Servers: web_server_1_1, web_server_1_2
Vpn Servers: vpn_server_1
Routers (FRRRouting): external_router_1, gateway_router, router1, router2
Topology: (gateway_router:eth2, external_router_1:eth0), (external_router_1:eth1, vpn_server_1:eth0), (external_router_1:eth2, web_server_1_1:eth0), (external_router_1:eth3, web_server_1_2:eth0), (router1:eth1, gateway_router:eth0), (router1:eth2, host_1:eth0), (router1:eth0, router2:eth0), (router2:eth1, gateway_router:eth1), (router2:eth2, host_2:eth0)

Your goal is to analyze the network condition and, if needed, use the available tools.
You need to generate a troubeshooting diagnosis report.
The report should reflect your assessment of the network's health, indicate any abnormal behavior you identify, and describe relevant findings based on your analysis.

Focus on producing an informative and coherent diagnostic report derived from the network state.
Do not need to propose any solutions or remediation steps at this stage.
